entry_points.role: primary loop — builds system prompt, resolves tools, calls LLM, drives processor
resolves native + MCP tools into AI SDK tool() objects
streams LLM events, handles tool-call/tool-result lifecycle, manages tool part state
calls streamText() from the AI SDK, routes through native or AI SDK path
prepares LLM request: system prompt, messages, tool filter, params
collects all tools (builtin + custom/plugin files), filters by agent/model
core tool types: Def, Info, Context, ExecuteResult, InvalidArgumentsError

session/tools.ts → SessionTools.resolve.output: Record<string, AITool> — AI SDK tool objects with execute() wrapped in Effect
session/tools.ts → SessionTools.resolve.wrapping: each execute() is wrapped with: semaphore(5), plugin triggers, Execution phase events, permission checks (via ctx.ask), truncation

session/llm.ts → LLM.Service.stream().calls: streamText({ model, tools, messages, ... }) at line 272 (AI SDK path)

dependency_graph.key_linear_path: prompt.ts:loop → SessionTools.resolve() → ToolRegistry.tools() (native tools) + MCP.tools() → AI SDK tool() objects → processor.process() → llm.stream() → streamText() → LLM events → processor handles tool-call/tool-result

how_tools_are_defined.pattern: Tool.define(id, Effect.gen(function* () { ... return { description, parameters, execute } }))
key_traits: Effect-based with Schema decoders for parameters
Context type provides sessionID, messageID, abort signal, ask() for permission, metadata() for UI updates
Return type is ExecuteResult { title, metadata, output, attachments }

how_tools_are_registered.pattern: each tool yields Tool.define() → ToolRegistry.layer initializes all via Tool.init() → added to state.builtin[] array

filters: ApplyPatchTool vs EditTool/WriteTool filtered by model ID (GPT models use patch, others use edit)
QuestionTool filtered by client type (app/cli/desktop) or flag

how_permission_works.pattern: Permission.Ruleset = Array<{ permission: string, pattern: string, action: 'allow'|'ask'|'deny' }>
two_layers: agent.permission — defined per agent (build=allow question/edit, plan=deny edit)
session.permission — from config, merged with agent rules
resolveTools() in request.ts filters tools out: disabled = Permission.disabled(Object.keys(tools), Permission.merge(agent.permission, session.permission))

how_agents_differ_in_tool_access.build_agent: default allows everything except doom_loop (ask), question (deny after merge — allow), plan_enter (deny after merge — allow)
how_agents_differ_in_tool_access.plan_agent: all edit tools denied except .opencode/plans/*.md, no plan_enter but plan_exit allowed
how_agents_differ_in_tool_access.other_agents: compaction, title, summary = all tools denied, hidden=true, used internally
how_agents_differ_in_tool_access.custom_agents: from config .agent.<name> with mergeDeep of permissions

key_test_patterns: no mocking preference — test actual implementation

significant_recent_commits: a1414d8c8 feat(tools): convert 27 plugin tools to built-in Effect tools with DuckDB backend
current_dirty_files: packages/opencode/src/session/tools.ts — Bus + Execution phase events added (uncommitted)
packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts — ToolCache, R type parameter added (uncommitted)

step_1_tool_registration.description: Each built-in tool is defined in its own file under src/tool/<name>.ts using Tool.define()

step_2_registry_collection.description: ToolRegistry.layer initializes all tools and discovers custom tools from disk

custom_tool_discovery.suffix: files export { toolName: { args, description, execute } } matching ToolDefinition

step_3_session_tools_resolution.inputs: agent, session, model, processor, messages, promptOps
actions: Get ToolRegistry.tools() — filtered builtin + custom tools for this agent/model
For each tool: transform JSON Schema via ProviderTransform, wrap with AI SDK tool() helper

Wrap execute(): semaphore(5) → plugin.trigger('tool.execute.before') → Execution.publishStarted() → tool.execute(args, ctx) → on error: publishFailed → on success: publishCompleted + plugin.trigger('tool.execute.after') with output + truncation
Get MCP tools: mcp.tools() → wrap execute() with permission check (ctx.ask) → same phase events + truncation + attachment handling
Start processor (msg = yield* sessions.createMessage → handle = yield* SessionProcessor.create(...))
await SessionTools.resolve({ agent, session, model, processor: handle, ... }) → Record<string, AITool>
Build system prompt from env, instructions, skills
Call handle.process({ user, agent, sessionID, system, messages, tools, model, toolChoice })
System prompt built (or sent via providerOptions for OAuth)
Tools filtered by permission (resolveTools in request.ts removes disabled tools)
streamText() called with { model, tools, messages, toolChoice, ... }
AI SDK manages actual LLM API call → receives streaming events
Events flow through LLMAISDK.toLLMEvents() → normalized to LLMEvent stream

events_handled.tool-input-start: ensureToolCall() — creates tool part in session with status 'pending'
events_handled.tool-input-delta: ignored (AI SDK emits final parsed input in tool-call)

tool-call.action: ensureToolCall() → adds call to toolcalls map → updates part state to 'running' with parsed input JSON
tool-call.extra: DOOM_LOOP detection: if same tool+input called 3 times in a row, asks permission
tool-call.note: The actual tool EXECUTION happens inside AI SDK's streamText — it calls the tool's execute() function automatically. The processor only sees the tool-result event after.

tool-result.action: readToolCall() → image normalization → completeToolCall() → updates part to 'completed' with output/metadata/attachments

tool-error.action: failToolCall() → updates part to 'error' with error message

step_7_result_formatting.description: Each tool's execute() returns ExecuteResult { title, metadata, output, attachments }
processing: MCP tools: content items (text/image/resource) are collected, images base64-encoded, text truncated
Built-in tools: result.output is truncated via Truncate.Service
Both: attachments get PartID.ascending(), sessionID, messageID assigned
processor updates the tool part with the completed state and output

build_vs_plan_agent_difference.difference_mechanism: permission filter in LLMRequestPrep.resolveTools() at line 188 of request.ts: 'Permission.disabled(Object.keys(tools), Permission.merge(agent.permission, permission))' — tools whose permission evaluates to 'deny' are removed from the Record before calling streamText

smoking_guns.finding: The MCP tools() wrapping in session/tools.ts replicates most of the same execute wrapper logic as the native tool path (phase events, attachments, truncation) but adds a permission check (ctx.ask) for MCP tools that native tools don't have natively — native tools rely on the permission filter in request.ts to remove denied tools before they're available to the LLM.

The `resolveTools()` function in request.ts filters tools by permission at the LLM request level, not at the prompt level. This means the tool IS visible in the prompt's tool descriptions but NOT in the actual streamText() call if denied. The tool descriptions are embedded in the tool objects themselves (from the AI SDK tool()) so the model sees them regardless.

External tool loading from .opencode/{tool,tools}/*.{js,ts} happens via dynamic import in ToolRegistry, but there's no separate loading path for external tools vs native tools — they're both Def objects, just with different schema creators (Zod vs Effect Schema).
